title: hypericum cassiopiforme
intro: Hypericum cassiopiforme, also known as the tree St. John's Wort, is a flowering plant native to the mountainous regions of Europe and Asia. It has been traditionally used in herbal medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antiviral, and antibacterial properties. The plant is rich in hyperforin and hypericin, which have been shown to have health benefits such as reducing anxiety and depression, improving sleep quality, and protecting against oxidative stress. Hypericum cassiopiforme can be prepared into herbal teas, tinctures, and infusions, which are often used to support mental health and well-being.
